_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: TX House HD109-113 (Dallas Co. delegation, state tier). Four progressive Democrats (Aicha Davis HD109, Toni Rose HD110, Yvonne Davis HD111, Rhetta Bowers HD113) + one swing-district Republican (Angie Chen Button HD112). Evidence from Texas Right to Life / Texas Alliance for Life / Texas Values Action scorecards, Choice Tracker roll calls, the 2021 SB1 Democratic quorum break, SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher) tally, SB14 (2023) crossover lists, Freedom Index, and direct candidate statements. DEVIATION FLAG: Button (R) is socially conservative (TAL 100% pro-life, TVA 87%) but a fiscal moderate (Texas Scorecard career 'F', Freedom Index 59%) who voted AGAINST vouchers in 2023 (flipped YES on SB2 2025) and was initially 'present, not voting' on permitless carry HB1927 before voting yes on passage. Cells with only party-inference or no individually-cited record left null.

profile.background: TX House District 109 (Dallas Co./DeSoto), Democrat, since Jan 2025. Former two-term Texas State Board of Education member (District 13); educator. Prominent public-education / anti-voucher voice.

aicha-davis@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-109 (D), since Jan 2025. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Strongly progressive freshman: self-described 'Planned Parenthood Legislative Champion' (100% pro-choice, PP Votes-endorsed); voted NAY on HB229 (binary biological-sex 'Women's Bill of Rights'); voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/school-voucher); Texas Values Action 2025 'Faith & Family' score 19%. Texas Alliance for Life rated her 48% in 2025, but that reflects the near-consensus SB31 'Life of the Mother Act' (passed 134-4), NOT a pro-life conviction. Not in office for the 2021/2023 votes (guns, border, elections, SB14) so those dimensions returned no evidence and are left null.

0.note: Self-described 'Planned Parenthood Legislative Champion,' 100% pro-choice; supports restoring abortion access — contrary to personhood-at-conception.

1.note: Pro-choice champion who supports expanding abortion access; opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Planned Parenthood Votes-endorsed 'Legislative Champion' — aligned with the abortion industry.

2.note: Voted NAY on HB229 (2025), which defined sex as binary/biological ('Women's Bill of Rights') — rejects biological-sex codification.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025) ESA/school-voucher program; former educator and prominent voucher opponent ('100% pro-public education').

profile.background: TX House District 110 (Dallas), Democrat, since 2013. Focus on criminal-justice and mental-health reform; authored HB1083 and HB3080.

toni-rose@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-110 (D), since 2013. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Consistent progressive: Texas Right to Life pro-life score 0% (2025), Texas Alliance for Life 45%; opposed defunding Planned Parenthood; voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher) and backed 2023 anti-voucher budget amendments; participated in the July 2021 Democratic quorum break that blocked the SB1 election-integrity bill (and the 2025 redistricting walkout). Did NOT join the four Democrats who crossed to vote YES on SB14 (2023 youth gender-transition ban) — voted with the Democratic bloc. Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade F. Several individual roll calls (SB8, HB1927, SB4 immigration, SB6 bail, Christian-liberty bills) were not individually documented — left null.

0.note: Texas Right to Life pro-life score 0% (2025); opposed defunding Planned Parenthood — contrary to personhood-at-conception.

1.note: TRTL 0%: voted against pro-life bills; opposite of abortion abolition.

2.note: Voted NO on SB14 (2023 youth gender-transition ban) — not among the four Democratic crossovers (Herrero, King, Dutton, Thierry).

0.note: Every present Democrat, incl. Rose, voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher); backed 2023 anti-voucher budget amendments.

1.note: Joined the 2021 Democratic quorum break that blocked SB1 (photo voter-ID / mail-ballot ID provisions).

3.note: Quorum break specifically opposed SB1's mail-in-voting restrictions, drop-box ban, and anti-harvesting rules.

profile.background: TX House District 111 (Dallas), Democrat, since 1993 — one of the longest-serving members. Committees: State Affairs; Land & Resource Management.

yvonne-davis@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-111 (D), since 1993. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Consistent progressive (YCT lifetime conservative rating 13/100): voted NO on SB8 (2021 heartbeat), SB4 (2021 abortion-drug ban), and HB1280 (Human Life Protection trigger ban) per Choice Tracker; Planned Parenthood Texas Votes-endorsed (2024); voted NO on SB14 (2023 youth gender-transition ban) — not among the four Democratic crossovers; anti-voucher record (YCT); participated in the July 2021 Democratic quorum break against SB1. Texas Alliance for Life rated her 45% in 2025, driven by near-consensus bills (Life of the Mother Act), not a pro-life shift. Gun/border/bail/Christian-liberty individual votes not documented (roll-call pages blocked) — left null.

0.note: Voted NO on SB8 (heartbeat), SB4 (abortion-drug ban), and HB1280 (trigger ban); PP Texas Votes-endorsed — contrary to personhood-at-conception.

1.note: Voted NO on the Human Life Protection trigger ban (HB1280); opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes (2024) — aligned with the abortion industry.

2.note: Voted NO on SB14 (2023 youth gender-transition ban); not among the four Democratic crossovers.

0.note: YCT-rated anti-voucher record; opposed school-choice/ESA bills (HB100 2023 lineage).

1.note: Confirmed participant in the July 2021 Democratic quorum break opposing SB1 (photo voter-ID provisions).

3.note: Quorum break opposed SB1's mail-in restrictions, drop-box ban, and anti-harvesting rules.

profile.background: TX House District 112 (Dallas Co./Garland/Richardson), Republican, since 2009. Chairs the International Relations & Economic Development committee; business/economic-development focus. Socially conservative but repeatedly flagged by fiscal-conservative groups as a spending moderate.

angie-chen-button@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-112 (R), since 2009. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Reliably SOCIALLY conservative but a fiscal/business MODERATE. Pro-life: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025), voted YES on SB8 (2021 heartbeat), backed SB33 (no taxpayer abortion funding); Texas Values Action 87% — supported HB229 (binary sex), SB1257 (detransitioners), opposed HB1738 (repeal of homosexual-conduct offense), supported SB12 (Parents' Bill of Rights), SB13 (library books), SB10 (Ten Commandments), SB11 (school prayer), SB965 (religious expression); YES on HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act). On guns: voted YES on permitless carry HB1927 final passage (NRA-PVF endorsed) but was initially 'present, not voting' on an earlier House vote. DEVIATIONS (broke LEFT of the GOP line): (1) voted AGAINST vouchers in 2023 / for anti-voucher amendments, then FLIPPED to YES on SB2 in 2025; (2) initial hesitation on permitless carry; (3) persistent failing fiscal-conservative grades — Texas Scorecard career 'F', Freedom Index 59%, voted NO on the budget (SB1). Election/bail individual roll calls not confirmed — left null.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025); voted YES on SB8 (heartbeat); backed SB33 (no taxpayer abortion funding).

4.note: Consistent pro-life record (TAL 100%); no abortion-industry funding documented.

2.note: Supported HB229 (binary biological-sex definitions) and SB1257 (Detransitioners' Bill of Rights).

4.note: Opposed HB1738 (repeal of homosexual-conduct offense); supported SB13 (library content) — TVA 87%.

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 (2025 ESA/school-choice). NOTE: she voted AGAINST vouchers in 2023 — a documented flip; current position is pro-school-choice.

1.note: Supported SB12 (Parents' Bill of Rights), strengthening parental notification/consent.

2.note: Supported SB13 (removing sexual content from school libraries).

3.note: Supported SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms) and SB11 (school prayer).

2.note: Texas Scorecard / Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade 'F'; Freedom Index 59%; voted NO on the conservative-preferred budget (SB1) — graded a spending moderate by fiscal-conservative groups.

0.note: Voted YES on HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act), a 10th-Amendment / anti-commandeering measure.
Voted YES on HB1927 (2021 permitless/constitutional carry) final passage. NOTE: initially 'present, not voting' on an earlier House vote — a documented hesitation.

1.note: NRA-PVF endorsed — opposes new gun restrictions.

profile.background: TX House District 113 (Garland/Rowlett/Sachse, Dallas Co.), Democrat, since 2019. Member of the Texas House LGBTQ Caucus; vocal gun-safety advocate; Vice Chair, Homeland Security & Public Safety.

rhetta-bowers@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-113 (D), since 2019. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Consistent progressive: pro-choice — voted NO on SB8 (heartbeat), SB4, and HB1280 (trigger ban), authored HB5087 (2023) to repeal the medication-abortion ban, PP Texas Votes-endorsed (2024); Texas House LGBTQ Caucus member who voted against SB14 (2023 youth gender-transition ban); voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher); participated in the July 2021 Democratic quorum break against SB1 (quoted at the U.S. Capitol urging the For The People Act); a vocal gun-control champion who calls rolling back permitless carry a 'top public safety priority.' Conservative indices rate her ~19-33%; TAL 50% (consensus-bill effect). Bail/Christian-liberty/border individual roll calls not directly confirmed — left null.

0.note: Voted NO on SB8 (heartbeat), SB4, and HB1280 (trigger ban); authored HB5087 to repeal the abortion-drug ban — contrary to personhood-at-conception.

1.note: Authored a bill to repeal Texas's abortion ban; opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes (2024); Reproductive Freedom Leadership Council member — aligned with the abortion industry.

2.note: Voted against SB14 (2023 youth gender-transition ban); Texas House LGBTQ Caucus member.

4.note: Texas House LGBTQ Caucus member — promotes LGBTQ identity in public policy.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher); publicly criticized vouchers for letting private schools 'pick and choose students.'

1.note: Participated in the July 2021 Democratic quorum break against SB1 (photo voter-ID provisions); urged the federal For The People Act.

3.note: Quorum break opposed SB1's mail-in restrictions, drop-box ban, and anti-harvesting rules.

0.note: Calls rolling back permitless/constitutional carry (HB1927) a 'top public safety priority'; opposes it.

1.note: Vocal gun-control champion; supports new firearm restrictions — contrary to opposing red-flag/magazine/AWB measures.
